magic_notes: Erdan rarely show magical affinity. When they do, it is almost exclusively Lightwielding — Clerics and Templars. A small number demonstrate minor Dark arts. They have no natural school mastery but can access any school at low potency.
unique_hook: Universal Vessel: No racial school restriction. An Erdan Cleric or Templar can grow into modest Lightwielding; an Erdan Scholar can dabble in any Materium school.

magic_notes: Skarn magic is almost entirely faith-based. Their Shamans are the exception — using Dark-adjacent blood rites passed down in isolation. Most Skarn are not mages at all.

school_scaling.Soulkindling: Moderate to High (when faith is strong)

unique_hook: Faith Amplifier: Soulkindling effects scale with Conviction score. A Northerner Cleric with high Conviction can reach High potency despite low Materium base.

magic_notes: Zai'Keth magic is practical and mobile — illusion-adjacent mind skills, short bursts of Bloodpassion fury, and rare Luminance use. Their Shamans appear in tribes of the Dark Forest outskirts, occasionally wielding dark arts.
unique_hook: Road Magic: Zai'Keth Mindwielding rolls in the open world (outside cities and dungeons) gain +10%. Their magic is tied to movement and open space.

magic_notes: Aelthiryn carry norium in their blood, making them natural magical conductors. Their school access is nature-centric. They cannot naturally access Shadow Arts or Void schools.
unique_hook: Norium Blood: All Materium-school spells cost 10% less Materium Connection. In forested biomes, spell regeneration increases by 20%.

magic_notes: Saereth have the broadest school access of any race — they can touch both Light and Shadow schools, though at half potency. Their strength is in Arcane and Geometric schools. No single-school mastery but unmatched versatility.
unique_hook: Broad Conduit: Saereth are the only race that can access both Light and Shadow schools simultaneously, albeit capped at 50% potency for those dual-aligned schools.

magic_notes: Velkaar are exclusively Shadow Arts practitioners. They cannot access Light or nature Materium schools. Their shadow potency is among the highest of any race. All Shadow school spells cost 15% less Materium Connection.
unique_hook: Dark Arts Only: Complete restriction from Light and Nature schools. All Shadow costs reduced 15%. In dark environments, Shadow pool does not drain.

magic_notes: Thyraen magic is cold, slow, and deep. Their Chill school effects are the most potent of any race. They regenerate Materium 25% faster in The Eternal Winds. Fire and hot damage counters them directly.
unique_hook: Frozen Conduit: All Chill damage dealt by Thyraen gains +15%. In The Eternal Winds, Materium pool regenerates 25% faster. Fire damage received +20% (vulnerability).

magic_notes: Gruthen magic is entirely shamanic and nature-bound. They cannot access cities' formal Materium schools. Their Bestial Communion gives them a passive healing tool between battles, requiring no pool drain.
unique_hook: Shamanistic Healing: Bestial Communion can restore 10% max HP between battles passively, without spending Materium pool.

magic_notes: Krothan have almost no Materium affinity. Their school access is purely combat-support — Blood Magic for injury-fueled power, Dominion for controlling weaker opponents. Most Krothan never use any magic at all.
unique_hook: Battle Surge: Krothan Blood Magic scales with current damage taken — the more injured, the more potent. A near-death Krothan Blood Mage is at their strongest.

magic_notes: Talarin deal the most Athropium damage of any race. Their Darkmind access is unusual — illusory and deceptive rather than aggressive. Animals are always neutral to them, giving passive Bestial Communion effects even without casting.
unique_hook: Nature's Apex: Talarin Athropium damage always rolls at +20%. Their magical insulation (20) makes them highly resistant to hostile Materium effects.

magic_notes: All Dornak have an innate affinity for Earthern magic — some develop into masters. Their Geometrics use is architectural and structural, not combat-primary. Luminance access is limited but present, allowing some Dornak Clerics.
unique_hook: Stone-Bound: Dornak Earthen and Terramancy rolls gain +10% underground or in mountain environments. Their connection to stone is literal — rock responds to them.

magic_notes: Urveth magic centers on Blight, decay, and poison. Rotweaving is their signature school, and they are among the best poison-infusion alchemists on Leonoria. Their Darkmind access is shadow-illusion, used for hunting rather than combat.
unique_hook: Venom Passive: Every Urveth attack — melee or ranged — carries natural venom (5% initiative slow + 1 HP bleed per round) without any Materium cost. All poison alchemy rolls gain +15%.

magic_notes: Ul'Karash are gifted in Earthern, Blight, and Fire magic — a combination born from their exile in the transformative Badlands. Their Voidreaving and Umbramancy remain from their Velkaar origin, though at reduced potency after the change. They are skilled general warriors first, mages second.
unique_hook: Ash-Born Resilience: Fire damage received is reduced 25%. Void Erasure (once per battle) removes a debuff from up to 2 allies. Purelight damage taken +15% (vulnerability).
